reasoning: User asks for indoor attractions in Kagoshima during a typhoon. Response returns get_spots which surfaces Sakurajima (active volcano, outdoor), Yakushima island (outdoor hiking), Jomon Sugi (full-day outdoor trek), and Kagoshima Castle ruins. None of these are indoor attractions and several are dangerous or impractical during typhoon conditions. The constraint — indoor, typhoon-safe — is entirely ignored.
missing_data: Indoor venues: Kagoshima City Museum, Shoko Shuseikan industrial museum, Reimeikan history museum, aquarium (いおワールドかごしま水族館). No indoor filtering exists.
improvement_hint: Add kind tags for indoor-capable venues (museum, aquarium, gallery) and filter by them when weather-constraint queries are detected. Alternatively expose an indoor_only flag or kind= parameter to let the agent pre-filter.

reasoning: Query asks for indoor rainy-season spots in Kyoto in Chinese. Response returns Nijo Castle (partially indoor), To-ji Temple (has indoor halls), Nagaoka-kyo (archaeological outdoor site), and Sanzen-in (outdoor garden temple in mountains). No indoor filter is applied. Nijo Castle and To-ji are legitimately partly indoor but this is incidental; outdoor-heavy spots are mixed in without any constraint handling.
missing_data: Kyoto National Museum, Kyoto Municipal Museum of Art, Nishiki Market, indoor areas of Fushimi Inari (covered portions), Nijo Castle inner keep details. Chinese descriptions absent — only Japanese/English names.
improvement_hint: Expose indoor_capable field and weather_constraint parameter. For zh lang, surface description_zh where available. Rank museums and castle interiors higher when rainy/typhoon queries are detected by intent classifier.

reasoning: User asks for cool spots in Nagano (highlands and caves) to escape summer heat. The search_hybrid query 避暑 涼しい returns STAY YAMAGATA portal pages (Yamagata, not Nagano), 涼を感じる (Shimane), and a camping article about Nagano but not caves. Prefecture constraint Nagano is completely ignored — top results are Yamagata. Caves (e.g. Ryugashido-like spots in Nagano) not surfaced at all.
missing_data: Nagano-specific cool spots: Norikura highlands, Tateshina highlands, caves such as Shinkashima limestone cave, Iida area caves, highland resorts like Karuizawa or Kiso valley. All missing.
wrong_data: Top results are from Yamagata and Shimane, not Nagano — misleading for an agent.
improvement_hint: search_hybrid with no prefecture_code filter cannot respect prefecture constraints. Either pass prefecture_code in the call or run a post-filter. Intent classifier should detect Nagano and add prefecture_code=20.

reasoning: Query asks for indoor Hokkaido attractions to escape summer heat (Korean). get_spots returns Showa-shinzan (outdoor volcano), Sapporo Clock Tower (indoor-capable), Matsumae Castle (outdoor), Daisetsuzan volcanic group (outdoor mountain hiking). Indoor constraint entirely ignored; most results are outdoor-heavy outdoor attractions.
missing_data: Indoor Hokkaido venues: Hokkaido Museum (北海道博物館), Sapporo Science Center, Hokkaido University Museum, Upopoy National Ainu Museum, shopping/amusement complexes. Korean descriptions not present — only name_ko field.
improvement_hint: Add indoor_capable or kind=museum filtering. Korean-language descriptions (description_ko) would substantially improve utility for ko-lang queries. Heat-escape intent should prioritize climate-controlled venues.

reasoning: User asks for museums or indoor cultural spots in Kanazawa on a rainy day. search_hybrid returns a museum list-page stub from Mizunami (Gifu, not Kanazawa), Kanazawa Kashi Kogata Museum (minor craft museum), an Omi Hachiman info page (Shiga), Kokuritsu Kogei-kan (National Crafts Museum, Kanazawa — relevant!), an Okinawa exhibition page, and Yukino Bijutsukan (Hokkaido — wrong region). Heavy noise from wrong prefectures; the key venue 21st Century Museum of Contemporary Art is completely absent.
missing_data: 21st Century Museum of Contemporary Art (金沢21世紀美術館), Ishikawa Prefectural Museum of Art, Ishikawa Kenritsu Rekishi Hakubutsukan, Honda Zouhinkkan. Critical Kanazawa indoor venues missing.
wrong_data: Several results (Gifu, Shiga, Okinawa, Hokkaido) are off-target and would mislead an agent composing a Kanazawa itinerary.
improvement_hint: search_hybrid needs a prefecture_code=17 or city-name filter when location is clear from query. Post-ranking geographic filter would remove irrelevant results. Kanazawa-specific Wikidata entities for major museums should be enriched.

reasoning: Query asks for covered/indoor Nara attractions besides deer in French. get_spots returns Mount Yoshino (outdoor mountain), Takamatsuzuka Tomb (largely outdoor archaeological site), Kofuku-ji Temple (has indoor treasure halls — somewhat relevant), and Koriyama Castle. Indoor constraint not filtered; temples with notable indoor museums like Nara National Museum are absent.
missing_data: Nara National Museum (奈良国立博物館), Todai-ji Daibutsuden interior, Yakushi-ji indoor halls, Horyu-ji treasury building. These are the obvious indoor answers but not surfaced. French descriptions absent.
improvement_hint: Add museum/indoor kind tagging for major Nara heritage institutions. French descriptions (description_fr) would improve agent usability. Rainy-day or indoor queries should trigger kind=museum,religious_building filtering.

reasoning: User explicitly asks for aquariums and indoor facilities in Okinawa during heavy rain. get_spots returns Nakagusuku Castle (outdoor ruins), Tamaudun mausoleum (outdoor), Shikina-en garden (outdoor), Taketomi Island (island requiring ferry — very impractical in typhoon/heavy rain). The explicit aquarium request is not addressed; Churaumi Aquarium is completely absent.
missing_data: Okinawa Churaumi Aquarium (沖縄美ら海水族館), DFS Galleria, Okinawa World (with indoor cave Gyokusendo), Southeast Botanical Gardens. Key aquarium explicitly requested is missing.
improvement_hint: Aquarium kind tag and indoor_capable field urgently needed. Heavy rain / typhoon queries should exclude islands requiring ferry transport. Taketomi Island recommendation during storm is a practical safety concern.

reasoning: Query asks for indoor Hiroshima attractions besides the Peace Museum (German). get_spots returns Arataniyama mountain (outdoor, best in fog — the opposite of what is needed), and the same spot duplicated in Chinese and Traditional Chinese. No indoor filtering; language requested is German (de) but content returned is English and Chinese. Zero German-language descriptions provided.
missing_data: Hiroshima City Museum of Contemporary Art, Hiroshima Prefectural Art Museum, Mazda Museum, Hiroshima Museum of History and Traditional Crafts. German descriptions entirely absent.
wrong_data: Returning zh/zh-TW language scrape entries for a de-lang request is a quality failure — the content is in the wrong language for the agent.
improvement_hint: Language filtering for scrape entries should prioritize lang=de content or fall back to en, not serve zh entries for de requests. Indoor venue kind tags critically needed for major Hiroshima museums.

reasoning: User asks about Hokkaido October all-weather (rain/snow-proof) spots. search_hybrid with query 全天候 屋内 returns STAY YAMAGATA (wrong prefecture, tourism portal aggregator), a Kumamoto coast route description, and a Nagano camping article. No Hokkaido results in top results; prefecture constraint completely unrespected. The Nagano camping article (雨でも楽しめる) is thematically adjacent but geographically irrelevant.
missing_data: Hokkaido all-weather venues: Asahikawa Zoo (open year-round), Moerenuma Park greenhouse, Hokkaido Museum, Sapporo Dome, Yubari resort indoor facilities. None present.
wrong_data: Top results are from Yamagata, Kumamoto, Nagano — all wrong prefectures for a Hokkaido query.
improvement_hint: Hybrid search must accept and apply prefecture_code filter. Without geographic constraint, general Japanese/weather queries return national noise. Add Hokkaido-specific all-weather venue tags.

reasoning: Query asks about Iwate caves for summer heat escape. search_area returns Ryugasendo (Iwate, exactly the cave requested), along with other caves (Ryugado in Kochi, Seiryu-kutsu in Fukuoka, Izumifukuji in Nagasaki, Gyokusendo in Okinawa, Oyachi-doukutsu in Yamagata, Daizakai in Toyama). The primary target Ryugasendo (Iwate) is at rank 2 with correct Wikidata data. Coverage of other caves is a reasonable broader result. Good match for intent.
missing_data: Specific temperature data (Ryugasendo is ~10°C year-round) would strengthen utility. No visit duration or access info for Ryugasendo in the wikidata result.
improvement_hint: Ryugasendo result lacks typical_visit_minutes and access details. Wikidata enrichment for this well-known cave would improve the response. Consider boosting Iwate-prefecture results when prefecture is mentioned in query.

reasoning: Query asks for Fukuoka museums or indoor spots on a rainy day (Thai). get_spots returns Fukuoka Castle (outdoor ruins), Mizuki (outdoor ancient fortification), Dazaifu (outdoor historical site), Genko Borui (outdoor coastal wall), Ono Castle (outdoor ruins). No indoor venues returned; all top results are outdoor historical ruins. Thai language not served — no description_th available.
missing_data: Fukuoka Asian Art Museum (福岡アジア美術館), Fukuoka City Museum, Kyushu National Museum (九州国立博物館 in Dazaifu — partially indoor), Canal City indoor attractions. Thai descriptions absent.
improvement_hint: Thai language support (description_th) needed. Rainy-day query should surface museum kind tags. Kyushu National Museum at Dazaifu would be a strong match — add museum kind tag to that entity.

reasoning: Query asks for air-conditioned indoor attractions in Osaka for August heat. get_spots returns Osaka Castle Museum scrape entry (relevant, has indoor description), duplicate in Korean, Garden Oriental Osaka (a wedding/restaurant venue, not a tourist attraction in the usual sense), Kudara-ji ruins (outdoor), Osaka Castle Park. The explicit air-conditioned constraint is not handled; outdoor spots are mixed in. Some useful indoor content present but diluted.
missing_data: Osaka Aquarium Kaiyukan, Osaka Science Museum, National Museum of Art Osaka, teamLab Borderless, Universal Studios Japan (covered indoor rides). Key air-conditioned venues missing.
wrong_data: Garden Oriental Osaka (wedding venue) is not a tourist indoor attraction — this is a misleading result.
improvement_hint: Add indoor_capable and air_conditioned tags. Post-filter outdoor ruins/parks when heat+indoor constraint is detected. Remove wedding/event venues from tourist spot results.

reasoning: Chinese query asks for indoor Fukuoka spots during typhoon. get_spots returns Fukuoka Castle ruins (outdoor), Mizuki (outdoor), Dazaifu (outdoor), Genko Borui (outdoor coastal wall). Identical to R-336 (same tool call, same prefecture) but in Chinese. Indoor/typhoon constraints completely unrespected. Chinese descriptions not present in the data — only Japanese names with English descriptions provided.
missing_data: Fukuoka Asian Art Museum, Fukuoka City Museum, Kyushu National Museum (covered galleries), Canal City Hakata (indoor commercial complex), MARK IS Fukuoka Momochi. Chinese descriptions missing.
improvement_hint: Same fix as R-336: museum kind tags and indoor filtering. Chinese language descriptions (description_zh) should be populated from translation pipeline for Wikidata-sourced entities in Fukuoka.

reasoning: Query asks for museums/art galleries in Tottori and Shimane on a rainy day. get_spots(Tottori) returns Tottori Castle (outdoor ruins), Utsubuki-tamagawa (outdoor preserved district), Yonago Castle (outdoor), Tottori Sand Dunes (outdoor — worst possible rainy day choice). Museums are absent; only one prefecture covered (Shimane not included at all). Rainy-day indoor constraint ignored.
missing_data: Tottori Prefectural Museum, Mizuki Shigeru Museum (Sakaiminato), Tottori Sand Dunes nearby Sakyu Center. Shimane entirely absent: Shimane Art Museum, Izumo no Okuni Museum, Adachi Museum of Art (one of Japan's finest). Critical omission.
improvement_hint: Multi-prefecture queries need separate tool calls for each prefecture. Museum kind tags urgently needed. Tottori Sand Dunes is among the worst rainy-day suggestions — outdoor sandy terrain.

reasoning: Vietnamese query for indoor Sapporo spots on a rainy day. get_spots(Hokkaido, municipality=札幌) returns Sapporo Clock Tower (indoor, museum kind — directly relevant), Sapporo Snow Festival (seasonal event, not indoor), Odori Park (outdoor), Clock Tower again (municipal scrape), and Entomology Hall (Hokkaido University). Reasonably scoped to Sapporo; Clock Tower is a good indoor match. Snow Festival listing is seasonally mismatched (rainy, not winter). Overall better than most weather-constraint cases due to municipality filter.
missing_data: Sapporo Science Center, Hokkaido University Museum, JR Tower Observatory T38, Odori underground shopping, Sapporo Factory indoor mall. Vietnamese descriptions absent.
improvement_hint: Snow Festival appears despite rainy-day query — seasonal mismatch. Add Vietnamese descriptions (description_vi). Municipal scrape for Sapporo has rich indoor content that could be better surfaced.

reasoning: Query asks about Japan Heritage or museum sites in Fukushima for indoor rainy-day visit. get_japan_heritage returns the Aizu Thirty-Three Kannon Pilgrimage story (relevant cultural heritage) with proper English translation and body text, plus wikidata heritage items including Oze (outdoor wetland — not ideal), Shiramizu Amida-do (a National Treasure Buddhist hall — genuinely indoor-capable), and two National Treasure objects (Ryukoji sutras, Katsujoji statues). The tool is appropriate; content is grounded. Tool scope note correctly flags UNESCO/national treasure distinction.
missing_data: Aizu Sengoku Museum, Tsuruga Castle (Aizuwakamatsu — indoor exhibits), Fukushima Prefectural Museum. Oze is an outdoor destination unsuitable for rainy days.
improvement_hint: Oze (highland wetland) ranks in top_canonical_heritage but is purely outdoor — add weather_suitability or indoor_capable signals. The Aizu heritage story content is strong; expand body_paragraphs to cover more specific indoor-visitable halls.

reasoning: Korean query for indoor cultural facilities and shrine interiors in Nikko on a rainy day. get_spots(Tochigi, 日光) returns Rinno-ji Temple (indoor halls with Buddhist treasures — directly relevant), Cedar Avenue (outdoor, but unique), Nikko Tosho-gu (partially indoor shrine buildings — highly relevant), and Futarasan Shrine. Both Rinno-ji and Tosho-gu have specific indoor structures that can be visited on rainy days. Municipality filter works well; results are geographically precise. Opening hours and website present for key spots. Good overall response for this query type.
missing_data: Nikko Treasure Museum (Tosho-gu Homotsu-kan), Nikko Museum, specific indoor hall names within the shrine complex would help. Korean descriptions (description_ko) absent beyond name_ko.
improvement_hint: Korean descriptions beyond name_ko would significantly improve agent answer quality. Add indoor hall details to Tosho-gu and Rinno-ji entries. Cedar Avenue should be flagged as outdoor/weather-sensitive.

reasoning: User asks about road closures and post-typhoon facility openings in Tokushima — this is real-time operational information. get_dmo returns a list of 4 DMO organizations covering the area with PDF plan links but zero operational status, no road condition data, no facility opening hours post-typhoon. The tool choice is inappropriate; DMO plan PDFs cannot answer this. The query fundamentally requires live data that the MCP cannot provide, but the response should at least clarify this limitation.
missing_data: Real-time facility status, road closure information, emergency contacts, DMO phone numbers for day-of queries. The tool_scope_note pattern used in R-341 would be helpful here.
improvement_hint: Add a scope disclaimer when get_dmo is called with queries about real-time conditions (post-typhoon, closures). Surface DMO contact information (website, phone) so the agent can direct users to live sources. Consider a no_live_data_warning field.

reasoning: Query asks about Hanaguri festival (花祭り) in Oku-Mikawa, Aichi. get_festivals returns 8 items: 4 designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties from Aichi (Kamezaki, Inuyama, Chiryu, Sunari festivals) and 2 fireworks festivals. Notably 花祭り itself — the Oku-Mikawa sacred ritual dance — is absent from results. The related festivals are legitimate Aichi intangible folk properties but the specific requested item is missing. Fireworks festivals are tangential.
missing_data: 花祭り (Hanaguri/Hanamatsuri) — the Oku-Mikawa ritual dance designated as Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property — should be the primary result but is absent. This is a notable retrieval gap.
improvement_hint: Verify whether 花祭り (Q-code) is in the bunka_intangible dataset. If missing, it should be scraped/added. Fireworks festivals mixed with intangible folk properties reduce precision; consider sub-type filtering.

reasoning: English query about Tono folk legends and kappa. search_hybrid returns Tono Monogatari no Yakata (遠野物語の館 — directly relevant, with rich description of Yanagita Kunio exhibition and storytelling), Tono tourism course (奇岩と石碑コース), DMO plan for 遠野ふるさと商社, and a kappa statue from Yamagata (broader context). The primary Tono-specific content including the folk museum is well surfaced. Score is high; the generic Iwate sports/strawberry listing at rank 1 is noise but Tono content ranks 2-4 strongly.
missing_data: Kappabuchi pool (カッパ淵), Denshoen (伝承園 — mentioned in the description but not as a separate spot), Furusato Village. Could be more specific about off-the-beaten-path character.
improvement_hint: The first result (iwatetabi.jp general listing) is too broad; consider de-duplicating portal aggregator pages in hybrid results. Kappabuchi should have its own Wikidata entity for direct retrieval.

reasoning: Chinese query about hidden villages and hiking in Shirakami-Sanchi (Aomori/Akita border). search_area returns Shirakami-Sanchi Wikidata entry (UNESCO WHS, primary hit), Akita Shirakami Tourism DMO plan with Fujisato/Happochi area details, Shirakami-dake mountain, and irrelevant Kumamoto entries (noise). The core target is found and DMO data adds substantive hiking content. Kumamoto results are noise. Chinese descriptions provided for some entries. Reasonable for this query type.
missing_data: Specific hidden villages (Fujisato-cho, Happochi-cho details), trail names within the UNESCO zone, Anmon Falls access. The query specifically asks about hidden villages — only mountain/area level data returned.
wrong_data: Kumamoto tourism entries have no relevance to Shirakami-Sanchi query.
improvement_hint: Geographic post-filter needed to remove Kumamoto results from an Aomori/Akita query. Add specific trail and village-level data for Shirakami area from DMO/NPS sources.

reasoning: Query asks specifically about Kumejima-tsumugi silk weaving in Okinawa — craftspeople and workshop visits. get_local_specialty returns 20 MAFF GI items and METI crafts for Okinawa but Kumejima-tsumugi is not in the top visible results; the data is dominated by food GIs (Ryukyu moromi vinegar, Gushichan peppers, Nakagusuku carrots, Chinsuko cookies, Okinawa kokuto sugar). Kumejima-tsumugi, if present, is buried below food items. Workshop visit information not provided by MAFF GI data format.
missing_data: Kumejima-tsumugi METI craft entry (if present), workshop/studio addresses, craftsperson contact information, visit availability. METI densan record for this craft if it exists in dataset.
improvement_hint: Add a q= or keyword filter to get_local_specialty so craft-specific queries surface the target item first. METI densan records should include studio visit information where available from official sources.

reasoning: Query asks specifically about Wajima lacquerware in Ishikawa — decline status and workshop visits. get_local_specialty returns 13 items but top results are sake brands and food GIs (Hakusan sake, Kaga Maruimo yam, Noto Shika korogaki persimmon, Ishiri fish sauce). Wajima Nuri lacquerware (輪島塗) — a METI densan registered traditional craft — is not visible in the truncated output. Food items dominate; the specific craft query is buried or absent.
missing_data: Wajima Nuri (輪島塗) METI designation record with workshop information, lacquerware hall (輪島塗会館) details, craftsperson contact. The post-2024 earthquake impact on Wajima craft community is not in the dataset but contextually important.
improvement_hint: q= keyword search within get_local_specialty needed so Wajima queries surface craft entries first. Consider separating food GIs and traditional crafts into distinct result sections or allow category_filter=craft.

reasoning: Korean query for hidden waterfalls and gorges in Miyazaki. get_spots returns Takachiho Gorge (prominent waterfall gorge — relevant but not hidden), Kirishima mountain (outdoor — not waterfall focused), Miyazaki Shrine (religious, unrelated), Obi Castle (unrelated), Mimitsu (historic townscape, not waterfall). Takachiho Gorge directly answers the waterfall/gorge part of the query though it is far from hidden. Less well-known Miyazaki falls absent. Korean descriptions beyond name_ko not provided.
missing_data: Less-known Miyazaki waterfalls: Manai Falls (真名井の滝 in Takachiho — world's best waterfall per TripAdvisor), Mentai Gorge, Mukabaki Shrine forest falls. Korean descriptions absent for meaningful agent use.
improvement_hint: Add waterfall-specific kind tags. Expand Korean description coverage for Miyazaki spots. The 'hidden' aspect of the query could be served by lower-prominence_score waterfall entities in the dataset.

reasoning: French query asks about abandoned or nearly-deserted villages in Shimane. get_spots returns Lake Shinji (popular lake), Matsue Castle (major tourist castle), Iwami Ginzan Silver Mine (UNESCO WHS), and Mount Sanbe (mountain). All are mainstream tourist attractions — the exact opposite of abandoned/deserted villages. The concept of 廃村 (abandoned villages) or 限界集落 (depopulated hamlets) is not present in the dataset at all. This is a coverage gap, not a retrieval failure; the query intent cannot be fulfilled with current data.
missing_data: Shimane abandoned village data entirely absent. Nima abandoned mining community areas, former Oki Island depopulated hamlets, any 廃村/限界集落 tagged content. French descriptions absent.
improvement_hint: Add 廃村 / depopulated-village kind tags if official sources document such sites. Shimane is Japan's most depopulated prefecture — tourism content around this theme (e.g. Ama on Oki Island) would be distinctive. French description support needed.
